questions.q: In which city is the Red Fort located?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort (Lal Qila) stands in Delhi, in the area of the old walled city of Shahjahanabad.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor built the Red Fort?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan commissioned the Red Fort as the palace-fortress of his new capital, Shahjahanabad.

questions.q: What is the Red Fort's local name in Hindi/Urdu?
questions.explanation: The fort is known as 'Lal Qila', literally meaning 'Red Fort' after its red sandstone walls.

questions.q: What is the Red Fort mainly built from, giving it its colour?
questions.explanation: The fort's massive walls are built chiefly of red sandstone, which gives it its name and colour.

questions.q: On which national day does India's Prime Minister address the nation from the Red Fort?
questions.explanation: Every Independence Day (15 August) the Prime Minister hoists the flag and addresses the nation from the Red Fort's ramparts.

questions.q: The Red Fort served as the main residence of which empire's rulers?
questions.explanation: For about two centuries the Red Fort was the principal residence of the Mughal emperors.

questions.q: What kind of structure is the Red Fort best described as?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort is a large fortress that also enclosed the imperial palaces, halls and gardens of the Mughal court.

questions.q: Beside which river was the Red Fort originally built?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort was built along the bank of the Yamuna river in Delhi.

questions.q: The Red Fort is the centrepiece of which historic walled city founded by Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan founded the new capital of Shahjahanabad (today's Old Delhi), with the Red Fort as its royal seat.

questions.q: What is the international status of the Red Fort?
options: An active military base off-limits to all
questions.explanation: The Red Fort Complex was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2007.

questions.q: In which year was construction of the Red Fort completed?
questions.explanation: Construction began in 1638 and the fort was completed in 1648 under Shah Jahan.

questions.q: Which grand gate is the main public entrance to the Red Fort, facing the Chandni Chowk bazaar?
questions.explanation: The Lahori Gate is the fort's principal entrance and the spot from which the national flag is unfurled on Independence Day.

questions.q: What was the function of the Diwan-i-Aam inside the Red Fort?
questions.explanation: The Diwan-i-Aam was the Hall of Public Audience, where the emperor heard petitions from the common people.

questions.q: What was the Diwan-i-Khas in the Red Fort used for?
options: Hall of Private Audience for nobles and dignitaries
questions.explanation: The Diwan-i-Khas was the Hall of Private Audience, where the emperor received select courtiers and special guests.

questions.q: Which famous golden throne, studded with jewels, once stood in the Red Fort's Diwan-i-Khas?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's celebrated jewel-encrusted Peacock Throne stood in the Diwan-i-Khas before it was later carried off.

questions.q: The white marble mosque built within the Red Fort by Aurangzeb is known as the:
questions.explanation: Emperor Aurangzeb added the small white marble Moti Masjid, or Pearl Mosque, inside the fort.

questions.q: A celebrated Persian couplet inscribed in the Diwan-i-Khas declares that 'if there be a paradise on earth, it is...':
options: this, it is this, it is this
questions.explanation: The inscription reads, 'If there be a paradise on earth, it is this, it is this, it is this.'

questions.q: What was the Nahr-i-Bihisht ('Stream of Paradise') in the Red Fort?
options: A defensive moat outside the walls
A water channel running through the palace pavilions
A reflecting pool for the emperor's tomb
questions.explanation: The Nahr-i-Bihisht was an elegant water channel that flowed through the royal pavilions to cool and beautify them.

questions.q: Which Persian ruler sacked Delhi in 1739 and carried away the Peacock Throne from the Red Fort?
questions.explanation: Nader Shah of Persia invaded Delhi in 1739 and took away the Peacock Throne and vast Mughal treasure.

questions.q: During the 1857 uprising, which last Mughal emperor was proclaimed leader from the Red Fort?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah Zafar, the last Mughal emperor, was made the symbolic figurehead of the 1857 revolt from the Red Fort.

questions.q: After the 1857 revolt, who occupied and used much of the Red Fort as a garrison?
questions.explanation: Following 1857 the British took over the Red Fort, demolishing many palaces and using it as an army garrison.

questions.q: Who is generally credited as the chief architect associated with the Red Fort's design?
questions.explanation: Ustad Ahmad Lahori, also linked to the Taj Mahal, is traditionally credited with designing the Red Fort.

questions.q: Approximately how long is the perimeter wall of the Red Fort?
questions.explanation: The fort's defensive walls run for roughly 2.4 kilometres around its irregular octagonal plan.

questions.q: Which earlier Delhi fort directly faces the Red Fort and was built by Shah Jahan's predecessors?
questions.explanation: Salimgarh Fort, built in the 16th century, adjoins the Red Fort and is part of the same World Heritage complex.

questions.q: In which year did construction of the Red Fort begin under Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: Work on the Red Fort began in 1638, when Shah Jahan decided to move his capital from Agra to Delhi.

questions.q: The exquisite riverside pavilion in the Red Fort, fitted with a perforated marble screen and a 'scale of justice' relief, is called the:
questions.explanation: The Khas Mahal was the emperor's private apartment, noted for its marble jali screen carved with the scale of justice.

questions.q: The pavilion in the Red Fort once known for a silver ceiling and a central marble pool is the:
questions.explanation: The Rang Mahal ('Palace of Colours') housed the imperial women and was famed for its decorated ceiling and central lotus pool.

questions.q: What was the Naubat Khana (also called Naqqar Khana) at the Red Fort?
options: The drum house where ceremonial music was played
questions.explanation: The Naubat Khana was the drum house, where musicians announced the arrival of the emperor and important visitors.

questions.q: The covered bazaar within the Red Fort, where luxury goods were once sold to the court, is known as:
questions.explanation: Chatta Chowk is the vaulted arcade just inside the Lahori Gate, originally a covered market for the imperial household.

questions.q: After India's independence, what change was made at the Red Fort to reflect the new nation?
options: It was converted into the President's residence
The Indian national flag was raised over it and it became a site of Independence Day celebrations
It was rebuilt entirely in white marble
It was relocated stone by stone to New Delhi
questions.explanation: On 15 August 1947 the Indian tricolour was hoisted over the Red Fort, beginning its tradition as the national Independence Day venue.
